deadlines.label: Register online or by mail
deadlines.detail: Last day to register to vote online at MyVote Wisconsin or by mail. After this date, you must register in person.

deadlines.label: In-person absentee voting begins
deadlines.detail: Early voting start dates and hours are set by each municipality. Check with your municipal clerk.

deadlines.label: Request an absentee ballot
deadlines.detail: Deadline (5 p.m.) to request an absentee ballot by mail or online for most voters.

deadlines.label: Register in person at your clerk's office
deadlines.detail: 5 p.m. deadline to register at your municipal clerk's office. You can still register at your polling place on election day with proof of residence.

deadlines.label: Last day of in-person absentee voting
deadlines.detail: End dates vary by municipality. Check with your municipal clerk.

deadlines.label: Election day
deadlines.detail: Polls are open 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. All absentee ballots must be returned to your polling place or clerk by 8 p.m.

explainers.title: Pick one party — and stay in your lane
explainers.body: Wisconsin's partisan primary is open: you don't register with a party, and every party's candidates appear on your ballot. But you may only vote in ONE party's primary. If you vote in more than one party's section, your partisan votes will not count.

explainers.title: Bring photo ID
explainers.body: Wisconsin requires an acceptable photo ID to receive a ballot, whether you vote early, absentee, or on election day. A Wisconsin driver's license or state ID card, U.S. passport, military ID, and certain student IDs all qualify.

explainers.title: What this primary decides
explainers.body: The August 11 primary chooses each party's nominees for the November 3 general election — including the first open race for governor since 2010 and the open 7th Congressional District seat that includes the Wausau area.
